title: INERATEC Advances PtL Project in Chile with Strategic Consortium
content: INERATEC has advanced its Power-to-Liquid (PtL) project in Chile by delivering the basic engineering study for a potential e-Fuel plant in the BiobÃ­o region. Collaborating with a consortium that includes ARAUCO, Abastible, and Copec (via Copec Wind Ventures), the initiative aims to produce sustainable fuels using biogenic COâ‚‚ captured from ARAUCOâ€™s forestry operations and green hydrogen produced by Abastible. Located at ARAUCOâ€™s industrial complex, the project leverages INERATECâ€™s modular Power-to-X technology to synthesize low-carbon fuels for mobility and chemicals. This development builds on initial concepts from the Energy Challenge 2023 and the H2Uppp framework. The basic engineering phase was fully funded by a grant from CORFO, the Chilean governmentâ€™s development agency. The partnership unites industrial emitters, hydrogen producers, and logistics experts to position BiobÃ­o as a hub for the emerging cleaner fuel economy.
author: Analysts Account
